title: Applied Data Engineering
source: Authored for the Salareen corporate catalog; structured to mirror the UK apprenticeship standard format.

knowledge.K1: The data engineer's role in building reliable pipelines and platforms.
knowledge.K2: Data pipelines and the difference between ETL and ELT.
knowledge.K3: Batch versus streaming processing and when each is appropriate.
knowledge.K4: Storage options: warehouses, lakes and lakehouses.
knowledge.K5: Data modelling for analytics, such as dimensional modelling.
knowledge.K6: Data quality and validation as a first-class concern.
knowledge.K8: Scalability and performance techniques such as partitioning, indexing and distributed processing.
knowledge.K9: Reliability, monitoring and cost control for pipelines.
knowledge.K10: Governance, security, lineage and privacy at scale.
knowledge.K11: Software engineering practices for pipelines: version control, tests and CI/CD.
knowledge.K12: How platform choices balance cost, flexibility and performance.

skills.S2: Implement ETL/ELT to move and transform data.
skills.S3: Choose batch or streaming based on freshness needs.
skills.S4: Select and model storage for different consumers.
skills.S6: Orchestrate jobs with dependencies, retries and alerts.
skills.S7: Apply partitioning, indexing and efficient formats for scale.
skills.S8: Monitor pipeline success, latency, freshness and cost.
skills.S9: Apply least-privilege access, encryption and lineage.
skills.S10: Comply with privacy regulation and document datasets.
skills.S11: Use version control, tests and CI/CD for pipelines.
skills.S12: Operate and recover platforms reliably and affordably.

behaviours.B1: Proactive in improving and automating data infrastructure.
behaviours.B2: Takes responsibility for reliability and cost.
behaviours.B3: Collaborates with data consumers and stakeholders.
behaviours.B4: Acts with integrity on data governance and privacy.
behaviours.B5: Operates effectively amid scale and complexity.

Duty 2.statement: Select and model storage for analytics.

Duty 3.statement: Ensure data quality and orchestrate jobs.

Duty 4.statement: Scale and optimise performance and cost.

Duty 5.statement: Govern, secure and protect data at scale.

Duty 6.statement: Apply engineering practices and operate reliably.
